Alythia macrantha is a plant species that belongs to the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the citrus or rue family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Alythia macrantha include flavonoids, coumarins, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, as well as to make essential oils and other herbal preparations.
